micro_segments.title: Rava's Inference from Ben Rabbi Chiya: Multiple Wrappings Are Necessary

Tosafot's Extreme Position: Even the Remaining String Falls Off

Why the Top Knot Must Be Biblical: Necessity of Binding the Strings

micro_segments.title: If an Entire String Is Cut Off at the Root, the Tzitzit Is Invalid

macro_segments.title: The Wrapping String and Its Count Toward the Four Strings

micro_segments.title: The Wrapping String Counts as One of the Four Required Strings

macro_segments.title: Proportions of Wrapping to Free-Flowing Strings (Kerach and Pil)

micro_segments.title: If the Majority Is Wrapped and Knotted, Still Kosher (Kerach Rubbah)
Even One Wrapping Section (Chulya) Alone Is Acceptable Post-Facto

micro_segments.title: Minimum of Three Wraps per Section; Range of Seven to Thirteen

Gematria Significance: God's Names and Echad (Unity) Encoded in the Numbers

macro_segments.title: Order of Wrapping: White First, Blue in Middle, White Last (Maalin Bakodesh)

micro_segments.title: Start and End with White: Maintaining the Primary Material Near the Garment
The Principle of Maalin Bakodesh: Not Descending in Holiness

macro_segments.title: The Case of the Completely Knotted Garment: Gedil vs. Pil (Knot vs. Strand)

micro_segments.title: A Garment Entirely Covered with Techelet and Knotted Throughout

Resolution: The Word Gedilim Teaches Four Strings; Pil Must Emerge from Gadil

micro_segments.title: Wool Strings on Linen Garments and Vice Versa: Both Permitted

Both Wool and Linen Strings Exempt in All Contexts (Potrin Bechol Makom)

macro_segments.title: Non-Wool/Non-Linen Garments and the Question of Biblical Obligation

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman: Silk Garments Are Exempt from Tzitzit

Resolution: If Biblical, Wool and Linen; If Rabbinic, Same Material Permitted

Two Pesukim Resolved: Wool and Linen Are Universal; Other Materials Only with Same
